title: persica vulgaris var. duplex
intro: Persica vulgaris var. duplex, commonly known as double-flowered cherry plum, is a variant of the cherry plum tree (Prunus cerasifera), characterized by its showy double flowers. The bark, leaves, and flowers of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Persica vulgaris var. duplex include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
